rds.globalcluster.CfnAbout:
The `AWS::RDS::GlobalCluster` resource creates or updates an Amazon Aurora global database spread across multiple AWS Regions.

The global database contains a single primary cluster with read-write capability, and a read-only secondary cluster that receives data from the primary cluster through high-speed replication performed by the Aurora storage subsystem.

You can create a global database that is initially empty, and then add a primary cluster and a secondary cluster to it.

For information about Aurora global databases, see [ Working with Amazon Aurora Global Databases](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraUserGuide/aurora-global-database.html) in the *Amazon Aurora User Guide*.

rds.globalcluster.DeletionProtection.Description: Specifies whether to enable deletion protection for the new global database cluster. The global database can't be deleted when deletion protection is enabled.

rds.globalcluster.Engine.Description: The database engine to use for this global database cluster.  Valid Values: `aurora-mysql | aurora-postgresql`   Constraints:  + Can't be specified if `SourceDBClusterIdentifier` is specified. In this case, Amazon Aurora uses the engine of the source DB cluster.

rds.globalcluster.EngineVersion.Description: The engine version to use for this global database cluster.  Constraints:  + Can't be specified if `SourceDBClusterIdentifier` is specified. In this case, Amazon Aurora uses the engine version of the source DB cluster.

rds.globalcluster.GlobalClusterIdentifie.Description: The cluster identifier for this global database cluster. This parameter is stored as a lowercase string.

rds.globalcluster.SourceDBClusterIdentif.Description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) to use as the primary cluster of the global database.  If you provide a value for this parameter, don't specify values for the following settings because Amazon Aurora uses the values from the specified source DB cluster:  +  `DatabaseName` +  `Engine` +  `EngineVersion` +  `StorageEncrypted`

rds.globalcluster.StorageEncrypted.Description: Specifies whether to enable storage encryption for the new global database cluster.  Constraints:  + Can't be specified if `SourceDBClusterIdentifier` is specified. In this case, Amazon Aurora uses the setting from the source DB cluster.
